description: This file defines the Past Continuous tense in Njo Viri (Balanda). The tense is used to express actions that were ongoing at a specific point in the past. It is formed using the auxiliary 'noka' and the verbal prefix 'ti-'.

general_principle.structure: Past continuous tense in Balanda is marked by the auxiliary 'noka' combined with the prefix 'ti-' attached to the verb. It expresses actions that were in progress in the past.

affirmative_structure.notes: Used to describe an action that was ongoing in the past. | Ne noka tidhe kümü; Ne noka timoliki gbi difo; Ni noka tinangele zanga; Uku noka timande gbi njü; Ne noka timerego ŋo

negation.structure: {"description": "Negation in the past continuous can be expressed using two licensed strategies.", "patterns": {"maka_gba_pattern": {"structure": "Subject + maka + ti-Verb + (Object) + gba", "examples": ["Ne maka timoliki gbi difo gba", "Ni maka tinangele zanga gba", "Afo maka timande gbi njü gba"]}, "kawilla_pattern": {"structure": "Subject + kawilla + ti-Verb + (Object)", "examples": ["Ne kawilla timerego ŋo", "Uku kawilla timele vongu"]}}}
negation.notes: Negation in the past continuous can be expressed using two licensed strategies.

questions.notes: Yes–no questions in the past continuous are formed by adding the question marker 'we?' at the end of the sentence. | Ne noka timoliki gbi difo we?; Ni noka tikulu munja we?; Uku noka timande gbi njü we?

negative_questions.structure: {"description": "Negative past continuous questions retain the negation strategy and add 'we?' sentence-finally.", "examples": ["Yehe ni kawilla tiriki dha we?", "Ne kawilla tiwutduru kese ngande’e ta kavokau we?", "Ni kawilla tiyele tamma we?"]}
negative_questions.notes: Negative past continuous questions retain the negation strategy and add 'we?' sentence-finally. | Yehe ni kawilla tiriki dha we?; Ne kawilla tiwutduru kese ngande’e ta kavokau we?; Ni kawilla tiyele tamma we?

verb_morphology.structure: The prefix 'ti-' must attach directly to the verb root in all past continuous constructions.
verb_morphology.notes: ti- cannot be omitted; bare verbs do not express past continuous meaning | timande (was working); tinangele (was running); timerego (was loving)

sentence_identification.structure: {"affirmative": "Subject + noka + ti-Verb", "negative": "Subject + maka + ti-Verb + gba OR Subject + kawilla + ti-Verb", "question": "Subject + noka + ti-Verb + we?", "negative_question": "Negative sentence + we?"}
sentence_identification.notes: Subject + noka + ti-Verb | Subject + maka + ti-Verb + gba OR Subject + kawilla + ti-Verb | Subject + noka + ti-Verb + we? | Negative sentence + we?

error_constraints.structure: Do not replace 'noka' with 'no'; Do not omit the prefix 'ti-'; Do not combine 'maka' and 'kawilla' in the same clause; Do not place 'we?' inside the sentence; Do not use bare verb forms for ongoing past actions

output: We were sleeping on the bed
They were walking in the compound
Uku was working in the garden
The child was playing in the compound

input: Wilii noka tikeye ti naa ni
output: The girl was calling her mother
The girl was tying the shoe
The girl was washing the shoe
I was contributing to the project
